tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Redb Architectural-Role Report
tasks.goal: The report proposes redb for embedded local storage, scratch state, embedding deduplication, buffering, and application-level WAL behavior. It places PostgreSQL plus RuVector behind redb for relational, semantic, and global state, and places envctl between ingestion and materialization. The report also over-attributes some application behavior to redb itself. Vector geometry, cosine similarity, SIMD execution, embedding generation, MessagePack ingestion, PostgreSQL synchronization, and microsecond latency are not established as redb-native behavior by the report. Those claims remain mapped to verification or owner-decision tasks.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Edge Orchestration with High-Frequency Buffering
tasks.goal: The source proposes an embedded redb hot-runtime buffer, background task-cycle flush into PostgreSQL, compression before commit, and redb as an application-level WAL during PostgreSQL outages. It does not define the implementation needed to make that safe: data classes, thread ownership, transaction boundaries, sequence keys, idempotency, compression identity, retry, backpressure, capacity, duplicate handling, conflict handling, or split-brain recovery. Its citation markers `[1]`, `[2]`, and `[3]` have no resolvable citation list in the indexed fulltext. The source therefore strengthens the use-case proposal but does not prove the latency, durability, or synchronization mechanism.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Architecting Nushell Plugin Integration with Redb and PostgreSQL
tasks.goal: The source proposes a Rust-native Nushell plugin using MessagePack and says the existing `FlexNetOS/nu_plugin` plus redb path removes the need for a new direct PostgreSQL plugin. It then presents redb as a parsed-code buffer and envctl as an already operating automatic synchronization bridge into permanent PostgreSQL storage. Those current-state assertions are not established by this source. Preserved planning evidence instead records an existing `PgStore` capability, redb as authoritative CodeDB source-blob storage, redb as envctl's durable migration database, and no live proof of the exact automatic redb-to-envctl-to-PostgreSQL path asserted here. The source therefore remains corroborating architecture input, not implementation verification or an owner decision. Citation markers `[1]` through `[4]` have no bibliography or exact source pointers in the indexed fulltext.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Architectural Integration of LifeOS and the Ruvnet Swarm
tasks.goal: The source describes a desired portable launch experience: LifeOS opens as the primary interface, the profile-owned Yazelix environment initializes Zellij and Nushell, Ruvnet agents start with `ruvllm` and `.rvf` state, and the LifeOS UI observes the swarm through UDS or shared redb state. It does not provide the installed-runtime, source-call-path, authority, concurrency, freshness, benchmark, or failure-recovery evidence needed to call that flow current. In particular, "UDS or shared redb state files" is an unresolved architecture choice, not proof that both transports can coexist without overlapping state. Automatic agent startup also requires the governed authority and lifecycle gates already represented in the task graph. The closing question asserts that an overarching swarm orchestrator uses Temporal Strange Attractors to forecast project timelines. No implementing source, model definition, workload, accuracy metric, or runtime proof is provided. Citation markers `[1]` through `[3]` have no bibliography or exact source pointers in the indexed fulltext.

tasks.goal: The source proposes direct database-native operating-system editing through SQL and RuVector hybrid search, guarded by MinCut dependency monitoring and Cryptographic Witness Chains. tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Cognitive Evolution: Redb Versus AgentDB Architecture
tasks.goal: The source proposes passive versus active memory as the primary architectural distinction between redb and AgentDB. The source characterizes redb as a passive general-purpose key-value store. The source asserts that redb retrieves data using static math. The source says any improvement around redb requires manual retraining of external models. The source characterizes AgentDB as an active self-learning cognitive container. The source asserts that one AgentDB cognitive container is packaged in a single .rvf file. The source asserts that AgentDB has built-in reinforcement learning. The source asserts that AgentDB autonomously adjusts its own weights. The source asserts that AgentDB autonomously adjusts ranking patterns. The source asserts that agent successes and failures over time supply the feedback used for AgentDB adaptation. The source implies a proposed role boundary in which redb supplies passive storage while AgentDB supplies active per-agent cognition. The source asks whether to explore an assertion that Ruflo uses the agents to predict future project timelines. The source asks whether to examine mathematical models such as Echo-State Networks said to be used for timeline predictions. The retrieved source contains citation markers [1], [2], and [3] without recoverable citation provenance in the fulltext.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Database Branching and Concurrent Code Management
tasks.goal: The system manages concurrent edits with copy-on-write branches inside the database. The system clones the current codebase state into isolated database staging branches rather than allowing agents to overwrite each other. Agents execute code rewrites within isolated database rows. Automated tests confirm that a branch is mathematically stable and successful before its rows are selected for merge. Rows from a selected branch are merged into the main trunk after automated tests confirm the branch. When a branch fails, the database rolls that branch back. Rolling back a failed database branch completely eliminates the dirty-repository problem. The source asks how the system mathematically prevents agents from bluffing or hallucinating during edits. The source asks how final code is exported. Citation markers [1], [2], and [3] are unresolved within the retrieved source and therefore do not verify the associated assertions.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Deterministic Timeline Selection via Agentic Temporal Branching
tasks.goal: The system guarantees execution of a winning timeline by selecting work already performed rather than asking an AI model to reproduce it. ATAS uses database-resident copy-on-write branches to clone current agent state into parallel isolated staging branches. Models perform actual work inside the proposed isolated staging branches. An Echo-State Network identifies a stable and successful branch, and that selected branch is committed to the main trunk. Merging a selected staging branch into the present completely eliminates the risk that a model cannot reproduce the desired outcome. The retrieved source contains citation markers [1], [2], and [3] without an accompanying bibliography or cited passages. The source asks whether to explore an assertion that RuVector MinCut acts as a real-time immune system isolating failing branches. The source asks whether to examine how the system forces truthfulness to prevent AI bluffing.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Edge Node Deployment and Agent Swarm Initialization
tasks.goal: Phase 2 deploys an agent swarm to local edge hardware through three milestones: Foundation Engine, Agent Deployment, and The Scratchpad. ruvllm is initialized on the edge nodes. A single frozen foundation model is kept loaded in shared memory on the edge nodes. Keeping one foundation model in shared memory conserves VRAM. Individual agents are deployed using AgentDB .rvf files. AgentDB .rvf files contain custom MicroLoRA adapters. MicroLoRA adapters hot-swap into the base model in milliseconds. Hot-swapped adapters give each agent a unique personality and skill set. redb is configured as an ultra-fast local AI scratchpad for agent state. Agents rapidly log high-frequency state changes and thoughts to redb without network lag. Citation markers [1] through [4] are present but their referenced documents or exact passages are absent from the indexed source. The source asks whether to move on to Phase 3 In-Place Refactoring and Immune Defense. The source asks whether to examine how AgentDB RVF files work.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Modular Intelligence: The Ruvnet AgentDB Framework
tasks.goal: The source asserts that each Ruvnet agent is deployed as one self-contained AgentDB .rvf file. The source characterizes each AgentDB .rvf file as a lightweight cognitive container. The source asserts that AgentDB containers run over one frozen foundation model hosted by ruvllm on edge nodes. The source asserts that each .rvf gives an agent distinct skills and personality through MicroLoRA adapter weights. The source claims MicroLoRA adapters hot-swap into the base model in milliseconds. The source asserts that SONA tracks an agent's successes and failures. The source asserts that SONA automatically adjusts agent behavior to avoid repeating mistakes. The source asserts that FastGRNN routing is a lightweight graph of agent memory that pre-filters what the LLM needs to focus on. The source claims that 50 unique agents can use the hardware footprint of one base model because of the modular structure. The source uses citation markers [1] through [7], but the indexed fulltext supplies neither source documents nor exact supporting passages. The source asks whether the final workspace is ready to initialize or whether AgentDB-container questions remain before beginning.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Orchestrating Rust and Node Separation with Gitkb Meta
tasks.goal: gitkb/meta acts as an orchestrator that isolates auto-generated Node bindings from clean Rust code. Defining clear boundaries in a .meta file lets Rust and generated Node-binding components be treated as parallel peers rather than a tangled monolith. meta exec can be configured to compile Rust only in pure directories such as ruvector-core and ruvllm. Restricting the Rust build to those pure directories bypasses JavaScript and WebAssembly output/exhaust folders. The restricted build guarantees a final release artifact untainted by napi-rs boilerplate. The source asks whether a comprehensive system prompt should be provided to instruct agents to verify and build this exact environment.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Orchestrating Vector Ingestion and Embedding Workflows
tasks.goal: The database itself does not generate embeddings during ingestion. The database stores and measures embeddings during ingestion. The system uses a local lightweight embedding model, such as all-MiniLM-L6-v2, inside a bare-metal Rust binary through an inference framework. envctl acts as the bridge for the embedding process. envctl pulls parsed structured code blocks from a redb buffer. envctl passes parsed code blocks through the local embedding model to translate text into high-dimensional vectors. envctl permanently commits both code and vectors into PostgreSQL. redb hashes text as a deduplication cache. The deduplication cache ensures inference is never recomputed for the exact same code block. The closing question asks whether to review Copy-On-Write database branching for concurrent agent edits. Citation markers [1] through [7] appear, but the indexed fulltext includes neither their source documents nor exact passages.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Portable Nix Builds via Musl Static Compilation
tasks.goal: The source asserts that standard glibc-based Nix builds put absolute /nix/store dynamic-library paths in executable RPATH metadata. The source asserts that an executable with those runtime dependencies immediately fails on a machine without the Nix daemon. The source proposes compiling the Rust stack for a musl target such as x86_64-unknown-linux-musl as a strict-static release route. The source claims that the musl route bundles all required dependencies into the binary, removes hardcoded /nix/store paths, and yields a standalone portable release. The source presents standalone true portability as the release outcome of the proposed musl compilation route. The source asks whether to explore gitkb/meta Rust/JavaScript build isolation or the LifeOS UI communication layer next; it does not itself assert either implementation. The retrieved source contains citation markers [1] and [2] without recoverable reference documents or exact cited passages.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Proportional Intelligence and Task Routing in RuvLTRA
tasks.goal: RuvLTRA routes tasks using a concept called Proportional Intelligence. RuvLTRA analyzes an incoming task's complexity and automatically routes it to the most efficient model. The source gives quick syntax fixes as an example of work sent to fast, lightweight models such as Claude Haiku or a local ruvllm node. The source gives massive architectural decisions or security validations as examples of work escalated to cloud models such as Claude Opus or GPT-4. RuvLTRA reviews parallel future timelines simulated by the system. RuvLTRA automatically steers the swarm toward the most mathematically stable and successful path. The retrieved fulltext contains citation markers [1], [2], and [3] without their referenced documents or exact passages. The source closes by asking whether to build the described components or review another architecture area; that closing question is not implementation evidence or owner authorization.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: RuVector Cognitive Architecture and Neural Database Logic
tasks.goal: RuVector turns PostgreSQL into an active, self-learning brain rather than a passive storage drive. RuVector natively combines vector search with Graph Neural Networks directly within PostgreSQL. The Self-Optimizing Neural Architecture tracks agent successes and failures. SONA automatically adjusts ranking patterns from tracked agent outcomes. Automatic ranking adjustment steers agents away from past mistakes. FastGRNN pre-filters data instead of forcing an LLM to read massive context windows. FastGRNN maps out exactly what an agent needs to pay attention to. FastGRNN filtering occurs before selected text reaches the language model. The system uses Cryptographic Witness Chains for truth verification. The system uses Causal Graph Verification for truth verification. Witness chains and causal graph verification mathematically link every fact or code edit to a real source vector. The verification mechanisms make it impossible for an AI to hallucinate or bluff. The source asks whether to review RuvLTRA task routing next or start building a selected subsystem. Citation markers [1] through [5] appear, but the indexed fulltext contains neither their source documents nor exact passages.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: RuVector MinCut: Deterministic Swarm Integrity and Logic Isolation
tasks.goal: The source presents agent communications and code dependencies as one mathematical graph consumed by RuVector MinCut. The source asserts that RuVector MinCut continuously monitors the graph. The source characterizes monitoring or MinCut identification as subpolynomial-time performance. The source asserts that the MinCut operation identifies minimum cuts in the supplied graph. The source maps graph minimum cuts to weakest links in swarm logic. The source asserts that corrupted or hallucinated agent output causes a measurable weakening of that agent's graph connection. The source claims instant weakening of the graph connection after corrupted or hallucinated output begins. The source asserts that MinCut detects the weakened graph point associated with the agent. The source assigns MinCut deterministic authority to sever an agent's connection. The source claims millisecond-scale detection-to-connection-severing latency. The source asserts that the workflow is rerouted after agent isolation. The source claims that isolation and rerouting prevent bad code from reaching the main codebase. tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: RuVector: Postgres Extension for AI
tasks.goal: PostgreSQL is described as an extensible object-relational database that permits custom data types, indexing methods, and functions. RuVector is described as a Rust vector and GNN database with a ruvector-postgres extension intended as a drop-in replacement for standard vector extensions. Installing RuVector inside PostgreSQL is proposed to eliminate a separate vector-database sidecar, external API calls, separate cluster, and duplicate synchronization/security operations. RuVector is claimed to expose more than 230 SQL functions covering BM25-vector hybrid search, graph analytics including O(log n) PageRank, and transformer attention. The source says an in-process RuVector extension can rely on PostgreSQL point-in-time recovery, RBAC, ACID transactions, and broad client-tool compatibility. RuVector is claimed to add 2-4 bit KV-cache quantization, 6-8x memory reduction, hyperbolic embeddings, and self-learning routing/ranking beyond pgvector. redb is described as a pure-Rust embedded key-value store without C bindings, running in-process with ACID and MVCC behavior. Vanilla redb is described as key-value storage without SQL, secondary indexes, native vector queries, similarity search, or GNN execution; distributed sharing would require an application protocol. Migrating to PostgreSQL plus RuVector is proposed to consolidate relational data, JSON metadata, and vectors and perform filtered similarity search in one network round trip. redb is not a compiler; rustc compiles it as a Rust crate and Cargo manages the build. Vanilla redb stores bytes and does not create embeddings; an embedding model creates vectors, while vector distance math requires a vector-enabled fork or application SIMD layer. The proposed workflow embeds source text and query text with a local model, stores vectors, and uses a database-side distance search to return nearest records. The source defines vector similarity as geometric distance and gives the cosine-similarity equation over high-dimensional coordinates. redb is claimed to obtain zero-copy mmap access and application SIMD using AVX2/AVX-512, processing 16 or 32 vector dimensions per clock and millions of operations per second. redb remains a general-purpose key-value database without any embedding model; an embedding model is needed only for semantic vector workflows. A tiered design is proposed with redb owning hot in-process state and PostgreSQL plus RuVector owning global long-term semantic memory and relational analytics. Agents are proposed to use redb as a local scratchpad and flush final compressed histories and embeddings asynchronously to PostgreSQL plus RuVector after a task cycle. redb scratchpad writes are claimed to take microseconds and impose zero lag on the agent loop. Edge nodes are proposed to batch configuration, telemetry, and keys in embedded redb during network instability and stream them to a PostgreSQL plus RuVector core for vectorization and relational indexing. redb is proposed as a BLAKE3-keyed cache of serialized vectors that bypasses embedding inference on cache hits and writes misses to redb and PostgreSQL. redb is proposed as an application-level WAL that buffers outgoing PostgreSQL operations during latency or outage and later replays them without losing a byte. The source assigns redb tasks to microseconds and PostgreSQL plus RuVector tasks to milliseconds. AgentDB is described as a serverless single-file .rvf cognitive container containing vectors, GNN indexes, learning state, cryptographic audit logs, and active reinforcement-learning ranking. An .rvf file is claimed not to replace foundation-model weights; ruvllm is described as the Rust/Candle inference runtime that keeps one frozen model loaded. One ruvllm model is claimed to serve many agents by hot-swapping rank-1/rank-2 MicroLoRA adapters from .rvf files in under one millisecond, with SONA steering subsequent generation. The source assigns global state to PostgreSQL plus RuVector, one shared frozen model per edge node to ruvllm, per-agent cognition to .rvf, scratch buffering to redb, and claims ruvllm writes updated memory back to each .rvf. The source proposes a local ruvllm/.rvf execution layer distinct from a Ruflo/ATAS/RuvLTRA global prediction layer that can use multiple cloud providers. ATAS/RuvLTRA are claimed to simulate branches with different providers and route tasks by a trained complexity classifier among local models, Haiku, Sonnet, Opus, GPT-4, OpenAI, and Gemini. ATAS is claimed to use Echo-State Networks to model codebase, prompt, and swarm-resource state as bounded Temporal Strange Attractors and detect trajectories leaving those bounds. ESNs are claimed to train only a linear readout and predict chaotic agent workflows in milliseconds on edge hardware. Parallel ESNs are claimed to simulate 10, 50, or 100 future steps containing projected git diffs, token/API/compute burn, complexity spikes, rate limits, and compiler failures, after which RuvLTRA selects the stable branch. ATAS is proposed to run real model work once in isolated .rvf COW branches and merge the selected successful branch rather than ask a stochastic model to repeat it. RuVector MinCut is claimed to implement fully dynamic subpolynomial minimum cuts, continuously identify weak links, and isolate/reroute failing agents in milliseconds. AgentDB is claimed to bind every fact, code snippet, and decision to SHAKE256 source-vector provenance and use GNN/Cypher causal edges to reject unsupported claims. The source claims RuVector makes it mathematically impossible for an LLM bluff to enter permanent memory or execution state. The source claims the GitHub monorepo is noisy generated build exhaust while Crates.io packages ruvector-core, ruvllm, and ruvector-sona are the clean pure-Rust source of truth and should be used instead of cloning the repository. napi-rs and wasm-pack are claimed to generate committed Node/WebAssembly bindings and multi-platform binaries, causing noisy branches as part of a prior-art publishing workflow. napi-rs is claimed to run the Rust vector/GNN engine in-process through native .node binaries distributed as an OS/architecture matrix without requiring end-user rustc. The Node binding is claimed to provide sub-millisecond queries and more than 52,000 inserts per second with zero network overhead. Bun is claimed to load napi-rs libraries out of the box, run bunx ruflo init flawlessly, and outperform Node in FFI/module startup. The source endorses the Yazelix/Nix/LifeOS/meta blueprint but proposes static musl compilation or bundled closures to avoid hard-coded /nix/store runtime dependencies. A Rust-native Nushell plugin using MessagePack is proposed to stream typed tables into .rvf/redb and avoid JSON/stdout serialization overhead. LifeOS is proposed to control/render Yazelix/Zellij through UDS or shared redb rather than HTTP, with gitkb/meta isolating Rust build boundaries from JavaScript/WASM outputs. The proposed final workflow is meta-managed source, Nix static build, portable launch, LifeOS UI, Yazelix Zellij/Nushell initialization, automatic Ruvnet-agent startup, and .rvf/ruvllm execution through native Nushell plugins. The user states FlexNetOS/nu_plugin is intended to support any database, is currently run/tested only with redb, and gives envctl import/export/environment-control capability. The response presents Nushell AST parsing, nu_plugin writes to redb, and envctl vectorization/synchronization into PostgreSQL as an existing flawless ingestion pipeline. The source proposes a PostgreSQL codebase table keyed by UUID with module path, block type, raw code, dependency UUIDs, and a 384-dimensional semantic embedding. Agents are claimed to find exact functions with RuVector vector/GNN queries, rewrite raw_code using SQL UPDATE inside COW branches, merge passing branches, roll back failures, and eliminate dirty repositories. envctl is presented as a materializer that queries the production database branch, reconstructs directories and source files from code blocks, and emits a clean Nix/Cargo build tree. The source claims hosting code in PostgreSQL solves agent context-window limits and creates a semantic IDE for autonomous generation. The user asks whether the proposed Yazelix/Nix/LifeOS/meta/RuVector ecosystem will be reproducible, portable, contained, distributed, complete, and runnable without the Nix store. The user asks whether .rvf replaces model weights, whether ruvllm is still required, and whether lightweight per-agent intelligence can be added without full model weights. The user asks how a one-frozen-model edge design relates to a multi-model/provider future-prediction engine. The user asks how a stochastic model repeats a selected future, whether MinCut provides the control, and how RuVector prevents bluffing. The final generated verifier prompt repeats many earlier source assertions as requirements, but repetition within this same chat does not provide independent verification.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: RuVector: Seamless AI Integration Within PostgreSQL Architecture
tasks.goal: PostgreSQL supports custom data types, indexing methods, and functions inside the database. PostgreSQL extensibility allows RuVector to operate natively within PostgreSQL as an extension. A RuVector PostgreSQL extension can execute vector searches and graph analytics through standard SQL where the data resides. Using RuVector in PostgreSQL can remove the need to run and synchronize a separate sidecar vector database. The proposed arrangement keeps relational data and AI logic in one place. Citation markers [1] through [4] appear in the fulltext, but the retrieved source contains no bibliography, citation targets, or quoted primary passages resolving them. The source asks whether to explore hybrid search combining relational metadata filtering with AI vector search, or other PostgreSQL enterprise features.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: Ruflo Meta-Harness: Adaptive Routing and Swarm Coordination
tasks.goal: The swarm coordinator is called the Ruflo meta-harness and organizes agents using Proportional Intelligence. Ruflo uses the RuvLTRA routing engine to analyze a task's complexity. Ruflo automatically assigns each task to the most efficient model. Quick syntax fixes or reflexes are routed to lightweight models such as a local ruvllm edge node or Claude Haiku. Massive architectural decisions or security validations are escalated to cloud models such as Claude Opus or GPT-4. The Ruflo meta-harness has authority to assign tasks among models based on task complexity. Ruflo uses ensemble simulations to make routing decisions. Ruflo spins up parallel swarms using different models. The parallel swarms debate, build, and test alternate future timelines. The source proposes steering the project down the safest path after alternate-timeline simulations. The source asks whether to explore Causal Graph Verification for truthfulness during execution or assemble a comprehensive agent-audit system prompt. Citation markers [1] through [4] appear, but the retrieved indexed fulltext does not include cited documents or exact passages.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: The Architecture of Simulated Future Timelines
tasks.goal: The source proposes that a predicted future timeline is a highly structured, localized state graph rather than a simple text summary. The source presents a simulated timeline as containing four named data points: State Diffs, Resource Burn, Complexity Spikes, and Failure Nodes. State Diffs are described as projected git diffs of the codebase at a specific future step. Resource Burn is described as a mathematical forecast of tokens, API dollars, and compute cycles that will be consumed. Complexity Spikes are described as predicting where a codebase becomes too complex for smaller lightweight models to handle. Failure Nodes are described as predicting the precise future step where errors are most likely to occur. The source says Failure Node predictions are based on past agent failure rates and gives API rate limits and local Rust compiler panics as examples. The source uses citation marker [1], but the indexed fulltext does not provide its target or exact supporting passage. The source asks whether the system guarantees that a swarm perfectly executes the winning timeline despite unpredictable stochastic models such as GPT-4 or Claude Opus.

tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: The Mathematical Guardrail: Cryptographic Witness Chains
tasks.goal: The source characterizes Cryptographic Witness Chains as a mathematical anti-bluffing mechanism. The source claims that Cryptographic Witness Chains keep AI truthful. The source asserts that agents store facts, code snippets, and decisions in per-agent AgentDB .rvf containers. The source asserts that every stored fact, code snippet, and decision is bound to a SHAKE256 cryptographic audit trail. The source claims that the witness binding is permanent. The source requires each LLM-generated claim to link directly to an original source vector. The source equates the original source vector with where the LLM learned the information for a claim. The source asserts that the system scans generated endpoints and code for a witness chain. The source assumes that hallucinated endpoints or code lack a historical root in actual data. The source asserts that the database rejects generated endpoints or code when the required historical witness root is absent. The source makes an absolute integrity claim that all such hallucinated artifacts are rejected. The source claims that witness enforcement forces probabilistic LLM output into a rigid deterministic framework. The source proposes exploring Causal Graph Verification as a companion for mapping witness-chain relationships. The source asks whether to examine Ruflo agent organization instead of Causal Graph Verification. tasks.title: As stated by NotebookLM source: The envctl Projection Engine: From Database to Directory
tasks.goal: envctl materializes database-hosted code into a standard file directory through a four-step projection process. envctl queries PostgreSQL for a final production branch before projection. envctl uses stored module_path metadata to reconstruct the exact directory structure in memory. envctl concatenates raw_code blocks into complete standard files, including Rust and TOML files. envctl hands the projected directory to a Nix or Yazelix build environment for final compilation. tasks.title: Coherent LifeOS experience delivered across user interfaces
tasks.goal: Expose the consolidated LifeOS environment through a coherent replaceable interface layer across desktop first and later approved mobile, glasses, and ambient surfaces without making the UI the system.
tasks.verification_gate: The phase graph separates Rust-native substrate from replaceable presentation, covers accessibility and cross-device continuity, and requires proof that every interface operates the same governed LifeOS state.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior experience plan; no application or device deployment occurs during planning.
tasks.next_action: After full graph coverage passes, decompose interfaces only after their underlying domain workflows are proven.

tasks.title: envctl database-to-directory materialization defined
tasks.goal: Define envctl as the deterministic projection engine from a selected PostgreSQL branch and version into a clean directory or build input, with no reverse ownership ambiguity and no manual edits to generated output.
tasks.verification_gate: A selected database version deterministically reconstructs paths, bytes, metadata, ordering, permissions, and module relationships; repeated projections are byte-identical; projection output is disposable and traceable to database identities.
tasks.rollback_plan: Delete derived projection output and restore the prior materialization plan; canonical database and source inputs remain unchanged.
tasks.next_action: Decompose query, projection, metadata, determinism, and cleanup into separate target tasks after graph closure.

tasks.title: Whole user-space parity recovery and authority cutover defined
tasks.owner_agent: Governance Board / LifeOS Data Agent / QA Agent
tasks.goal: Define the staged migration that proves the entire user-space is represented, reconstructable, recoverable, and operated from PostgreSQL before retiring flat files as editable authority while retaining preserved history and projections.
tasks.verification_gate: Entity counts, identities, bytes, metadata, relationships, permissions, history, semantic retrieval, behavior, reconstruction, backup, restore, failback, and observed LifeOS operation all pass before one explicit authority switch; preserved source remains available until retention approval.
tasks.rollback_plan: Fail back to preserved source authority, stop database writes, and retain the failed cutover namespace and proof without deleting either representation.
tasks.next_action: Keep authority unchanged; after graph closure decompose parity, restore rehearsal, staged dual-read, explicit switch, and retention decisions.

tasks.next_action: Write the bounded ingestion fixtures and failing protocol, provenance, and deduplication tests.

tasks.title: Implement the redb outbox and PostgreSQL RuVector synchronization seam
tasks.goal: Use TDD to make redb the restartable local buffer and explicit application outbox for ordered embedding work, then synchronize idempotently into PostgreSQL plus RuVector without shared-store or authority ambiguity.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests cover crash at every enqueue, embed, flush, and acknowledge boundary; replay is ordered, idempotent, observable, and lossless; model/version provenance is recorded; PostgreSQL remains macro-state authority and envctl consumes only a versioned export contract.
tasks.rollback_plan: Disable the synchronizer, replay the append-only outbox from the last acknowledged cursor, remove only task-created test namespaces, and restore the prior adapter version.
tasks.next_action: Begin after ARCHBP-001 proves exact native ingestion.

tasks.title: Build the live RuVector semantic and hybrid SQL adapter

tasks.next_action: Select the profile-owned embedding model and write failing adapter and hybrid-query tests after ARCHBP-002.

tasks.title: Build the envctl production projection engine
tasks.goal: Use TDD to query a selected approved database branch and deterministically project .rs, .toml, and .nu files with exact bytes, module_path metadata, permissions, safe links, ordering, and disposable output ownership.

tasks.rollback_plan: Delete the derived projection by its receipt, restore the prior projection adapter, and leave source rows, canonical source, and installed runtime unchanged.
tasks.next_action: Define the versioned export contract and failing exact-projection fixtures.

tasks.title: Integrate ruvllm shared-model edge inference
tasks.goal: Use TDD to run profile-owned ruvllm inference with a shared frozen base model and isolated RVF cartridges, proving startup, adapter selection, bounded resource ownership, local fallback, and measured latency without asserting unsupported hot-swap performance.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests prove one frozen model identity, isolated cartridge state, deterministic startup and shutdown, bounded CPU/GPU/RAM use, local failure behavior, and raw latency samples; any adapter hot-swap claim is limited to the measured workload.
tasks.rollback_plan: Stop the inference service, remove task-created model adapters and RVF fixtures, restore the prior profile package, and retain benchmark and failure receipts.
tasks.next_action: Select the exact profile-owned ruvllm artifact and write failing cartridge lifecycle tests.

tasks.title: Prove MicroLoRA SONA and FastGRNN learning boundaries
tasks.goal: Use TDD to integrate only primary-source-supported MicroLoRA, SONA, and FastGRNN components, bind learning state to agent identity and witness provenance, and prove rollback, resource limits, and promotion gates before learned state affects execution.

tasks.rollback_plan: Disable learned-state promotion, restore the frozen base adapter, quarantine task-created learning state, and keep provenance and rejection evidence.

tasks.title: Prove swarm scale and hardware acceptance
tasks.goal: Replace the blueprint's 50-plus-agent and fixed-latency assertions with reproducible scale tests across declared CPU, GPU, memory, storage, and network cells, including overload, degradation, fairness, recovery, and cost envelopes.
tasks.verification_gate: The harness publishes raw samples and exact hardware/software identities; runs 1, 8, 16, 32, and at least 50 agents when capacity permits; exercises overload and recovery; reports confidence intervals and honest limits; no architecture claim exceeds measured evidence.
tasks.rollback_plan: Stop the load cell, remove task-created state, restore resource limits, and retain raw failure samples and capacity findings.
tasks.next_action: Define the workload, resource budgets, failure cases, and red acceptance thresholds.

tasks.title: Build the ATAS ESN future-timeline research harness
tasks.goal: Use TDD research to build a bounded Agentic Temporal Attractor Studio harness with echo-state reservoirs, ensemble simulations, state diffs, resource burn, complexity spikes, and failure-node outputs without promoting forecasts to authority.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests establish deterministic seeded runs, leakage-free datasets, naive baselines, reservoir/readout separation, ensemble outputs, calibrated uncertainty, and explicit non-authority labeling for every timeline.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove task-created models and datasets, retain raw evaluation results, and restore the prior research adapter; no operational route changes.
tasks.next_action: Verify primary algorithms and define the leakage-free dataset, naive baselines, and failing forecast tests.

tasks.title: Validate temporal strange-attractor claims empirically
tasks.owner_agent: Predictive Systems Agent / Research QA Agent
tasks.goal: Determine whether stable attractor-like structures actually appear in the declared software-state embedding and whether they improve out-of-sample forecast or decision quality beyond simpler baselines.
tasks.verification_gate: Pre-registered tests compare attractor metrics against shuffled and simple-model baselines across seeds; findings report negative as well as positive results; only reproducible out-of-sample gains permit downstream use.
tasks.rollback_plan: Delete task-created derived models and plots, preserve raw data and negative results, and keep routing disabled if the hypothesis fails.
tasks.next_action: Pre-register the attractor, robustness, null-hypothesis, and out-of-sample tests.

tasks.title: Implement RuvLTRA proportional local-cloud routing
tasks.goal: Use TDD to route workloads proportionally between local and approved cloud execution from measured complexity, confidence, privacy, cost, and resource signals, with an explicit local-only default and no route based solely on forecast prose.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests cover local-only default, cost ceiling, privacy denial, resource exhaustion, classifier uncertainty, outage fallback, and auditability; offline replay beats declared baselines before any live route is enabled.
tasks.rollback_plan: Disable cloud routing, force local-only policy, revoke task-scoped credentials, restore the prior classifier, and retain routing decisions and cost evidence.
tasks.next_action: Write failing local-default, privacy, cost, uncertainty, and outage-routing tests.

tasks.title: Integrate RuVector MinCut dynamic swarm isolation
tasks.goal: Use TDD to detect and isolate harmful or inconsistent swarm edges using the exact supported MinCut implementation, with bounded complexity, explainable cut evidence, false-positive analysis, recovery, and no claim of subpolynomial behavior without proof.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests cover compromised, noisy, partitioned, and healthy swarms; every cut has reproducible evidence and bounded blast radius; false positives and recovery are measured; complexity wording matches benchmark and primary implementation.
tasks.rollback_plan: Disable automatic isolation, restore the prior graph, reattach only receipt-identified healthy edges after review, and retain attack and false-positive evidence.
tasks.next_action: Build the attack corpus and write failing isolation, false-positive, recovery, and complexity tests.

tasks.title: Implement witness-chain anti-bluffing receipts
tasks.goal: Use TDD and primary cryptographic guidance to bind source, vector, decision, execution, and proof events into tamper-evident witness chains; decide SHAKE256 versus the currently proven digest scheme explicitly instead of copying the blueprint algorithm by assertion.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests detect event removal, reorder, substitution, replay, algorithm confusion, and partial writes; the chosen primitive and domain separation are documented; verification is independent; claims are limited to tamper evidence and fail-closed policy.
tasks.rollback_plan: Disable the new writer, preserve and verify the prior ledger, restore the previous schema, and retain task-created invalid-chain fixtures and migration evidence.
tasks.next_action: Record the algorithm decision and write failing tamper, reorder, replay, and partial-write tests.

tasks.title: Enforce causal graph GNN and Cypher authorization guardrails
tasks.goal: Use TDD to construct causal and dependency graphs from provenance-bound evidence, evaluate supported GNN signals and Cypher queries as advisory detectors, and require deterministic policy plus witness evidence before any mutation is authorized.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests cover missing, forged, cyclic, stale, and contradictory edges; Cypher results trace to exact evidence; GNN metrics are compared with baselines; deterministic approval policy remains final and every allow or deny has a witness receipt.
tasks.rollback_plan: Disable the advisory model and queries, restore deterministic approval-only behavior, remove task-created derived graph rows, and retain adversarial findings.
tasks.next_action: Define the evidence-bound causal schema and failing forgery, staleness, contradiction, and authorization tests.

tasks.title: Prove the LifeOS Yazelix UDS and shared-state ownership contract
tasks.goal: Use TDD to select and prove the LifeOS-to-Yazelix control transport, preferring a versioned authenticated Unix-domain socket unless an explicit shared-redb design proves single-writer ownership, locking, recovery, and upgrade compatibility.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests cover peer authentication, permissions, version skew, restart, concurrent clients, stale socket cleanup, malformed frames, and ownership; installed proof enters through profile yzx and no component silently shares redb write authority.
tasks.rollback_plan: Stop the task-created service, remove its socket by receipt, restore the prior profile generation and protocol adapter, and preserve config inputs and state databases.
tasks.next_action: Record the UDS-versus-shared-store decision and write failing ownership, authentication, compatibility, and recovery tests.

tasks.title: Prove full-stack Yazelix musl and portable-closure release
tasks.goal: Execute the detailed YZXCONV-021 task: prove x86_64-unknown-linux-musl for eligible binaries, classify the non-musl GUI and Nix closure, select a closure-extract, bundle, AppImage-like, nix-user-chroot, or documented host-Nix fallback, and prove a relocatable launch folder without false no-/nix/store claims.

tasks.next_action: Run the YZXCONV-021 red-test design and portability matrix as the detailed owning task.

tasks.title: Make GitKB and Meta release isolation branch-current
tasks.goal: Configure explicit repository discovery and branch-current GitKB code indexes for the relevant peers, preserve Meta coordination without monorepo fusion, and prove that release decisions resolve exact repositories and commits rather than stale or empty indexes.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests fail on empty, stale, wrong-branch, or ambiguous repository indexes; current indexes resolve the exact peers and commits used by release tasks; Meta preserves independent lockfiles and repos; index state is never treated as source authority.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove task-created indexes and repository entries, restore the prior KB config, and keep releases blocked until a current index can be rebuilt.
tasks.next_action: Define the repository set and write failing empty, stale, wrong-branch, and ambiguity tests.

tasks.title: Round-trip TEAS work orders status and proof through GitKB
tasks.goal: Use TDD to eliminate the TEAS-to-GitKB write-back seam so canonical task bodies, lifecycle status, and proof references appear consistently in GitKB board, graph, ready, assign, context, and incident views without dual authority.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests cover creation, assignment, running, blocked, complete, proof invalidation, retry, conflict, and restart; one authority is explicit; GitKB views and TEAS agree after replay; contradictions fail closed and remain visible.
tasks.rollback_plan: Disable the adapter, replay from the last common receipt, restore prior task documents and TEAS rows, and keep contradictory tasks blocked.
tasks.next_action: Choose the authoritative direction and write failing lifecycle, replay, conflict, and invalidation round-trip tests.

tasks.title: Build CodeDB single-binary export and run full peer consolidation
tasks.goal: Use TDD to implement the bounded CodeDB snapshot export crate and execute a provenance-bound all-peer consolidation rehearsal that preserves independent source repos while producing one reviewable LifeOS release artifact.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests prove snapshot verify, list, materialize, license, corruption rejection, deterministic build, and rollback; the rehearsal accounts for every peer capability and preserves each independent repository and lockfile; no release claim exceeds the bounded artifact.
tasks.rollback_plan: Delete task-created export and rehearsal artifacts by manifest, restore the prior exporter source, and leave peer repositories and canonical release untouched.
tasks.next_action: Write failing exporter integrity, determinism, license, materialization, and rollback tests before the all-peer rehearsal.

tasks.title: Produce exact-clean-tree public release and detached attestation proof
tasks.goal: Prove a public release from an exact clean commit with reproducible artifact hashes, source and toolchain provenance, SBOM and license completeness, detached attestation, clean runner state, rollback, and post-publication verification.
tasks.verification_gate: Two clean builds of the same commit produce the declared identical artifacts; repository, submodule, lock, toolchain, runner, SBOM, license, and checksum identities are bound; detached attestation verifies independently; rollback and compromise procedures execute in the isolated cell.
tasks.rollback_plan: Revoke and mark the test release invalid, delete only task-created public artifacts where policy permits, restore no mutable tag silently, and retain the incident and attestation evidence.
tasks.next_action: Define the clean-release manifest and failing dirty-tree, provenance, license, attestation, and rollback tests.

tasks.title: Prove Home Manager desktop and profile startup ownership
tasks.owner_agent: Yazelix Home Manager Agent / Desktop Agent
tasks.goal: Execute the detailed YZXCONV-022 task: keep Home Manager inside the one foundation element, materialize reviewed config inputs, launch profile yzx with the profile Zellij layout, start declared LifeOS and agent services, and remove stale user-local binary and desktop shadows.
tasks.verification_gate: YZXCONV-022 proof is current; Nix evaluation and build show one foundation element; the visible desktop entry launches profile yzx with the profile layout; declared background services start and stop cleanly; stale local shadows are absent; generated runtime matches reviewed inputs.
tasks.rollback_plan: Reselect the recorded prior profile generation, restore only reviewed editable inputs, stop task-created services, and never restore stale shadows or edit generated runtime.
tasks.next_action: Run YZXCONV-022 as the detailed owning task and link its proof into the root ledger.

tasks.title: Restore durable RULES.md authority and generated projection
tasks.goal: Create the missing durable operating-rules source at the documented canonical path, give envctl and Yazelix one reviewed projection route, and add negative tests preventing retired workspace mirrors, generated runtime, caches, or marketplace copies from becoming active authority.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests fail when RULES.md is missing, duplicated, stale, or sourced from retired mirrors; preview is idempotent; installed checksum traces to one reviewed editable source through envctl and the profile without widening plugins or MCPs.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior source and projection receipts, remove only the task-created projection if invalid, and keep agent execution blocked when durable rules are absent.

tasks.title: Wire Meta exec to dependency-DAG execution order
tasks.goal: Use TDD to make meta exec execute dependency levels sequentially and peers within a level in parallel, preserving independent repositories, deterministic display, filters, failure propagation, cancellation, and cycle rejection.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests prove dependencies finish before dependents, same-level peers respect the parallel cap, tag filters preserve topology, cycles and unresolved required dependencies fail closed, and cancellation prevents later levels from starting.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior alphabetical executor, disable dependency-sensitive fleet actions, and retain failing topology and cancellation evidence.
tasks.next_action: Write failing multi-level, parallel-level, filter, cycle, failure, and cancellation tests.

tasks.title: Decide Rust toolchain bundling versus build-only provenance
tasks.goal: Record a bounded owner decision selecting a relocatable Rust closure inside the release prefix or build-only provenance, with size, licensing, offline rebuild, security-update, rollback, and host-dependency consequences.
tasks.verification_gate: The owner records one option or an explicitly bounded hybrid, exact unblock conditions, provenance and update ownership, offline and relocation expectations, rollback, and effects on musl and closure tasks; no release task assumes an undecided model.
tasks.rollback_plan: Reopen the decision, restore the prior release contract, and keep dependent release tasks blocked; no toolchain or profile state changes.
tasks.next_action: Prepare the option matrix and request the bounded owner decision.

tasks.title: Complete envctl root relocation and retire the legacy toolchain bridge
tasks.goal: Use TDD to make the envctl-owned portable prefix the canonical post-bootstrap runtime root, retire compatibility .toolchains and user-local binary shadows, preserve the one Yazelix profile as current install owner during transition, and prove rollback without split ownership.

tasks.next_action: Write failing canonical-root, split-owner, migration, upgrade, and rollback tests before moving any frontdoor.

tasks.title: Consolidate guard-bash and Meta agent guard policy ownership
tasks.goal: Use TDD to inventory overlapping guard-bash and meta agent guard behavior, choose one policy source with explicit adapters where required, eliminate contradictory enforcement, and preserve Nushell-only agent execution plus owner-only emergency fallback.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests expose every overlap and contradiction; one source owns each rule; adapters are traceable; Nushell-only and crash-specific owner approval remain enforced; removal of either legacy route cannot silently disable a control.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior guard configuration from receipts, disable agents if enforcement is ambiguous, and retain the contradiction report until one owner is re-established.
tasks.next_action: Build the overlap matrix and write failing duplicate, contradictory, missing-rule, and alternate-shell tests.

tasks.title: Prevent accidental vault_hub raw-secret commits
tasks.goal: Add and test repository-owned ignore and pre-commit controls for vault_keeper, .ssh, .env, live KeePass, and other raw-secret paths without reading secret values, and move real material outside the repository when ownership permits.
tasks.verification_gate: Tests use synthetic filenames and values only; git status and check-ignore prove every raw-secret path class is excluded; pre-commit rejects forced additions; no secret contents enter logs, proof, Git history, or task artifacts.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior ignore and hook files, keep the repository blocked from broad add operations, and never move or delete real secret material without explicit owner approval.
tasks.next_action: Write synthetic failing check-ignore and forced-add rejection tests without reading any real secret value.

tasks.goal: Use TDD to make strict documentation verification ignore inline-code link examples, resolve wiki aliases through the same maintained navigation index as the generator, preserve repository-relative path safety, and still reject genuinely missing or escaping targets.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests reproduce the inline Markdown example false positive and bare wiki-alias false negative; code spans and fenced examples are ignored; aliases resolve exactly as the generated index; missing, ambiguous, absolute-escape, and parent-escape targets still fail; planning verification and navigation checks agree.

tasks.title: Reconcile verifier failure state with proof and task status projections
tasks.owner_agent: Planning Spine Proof Agent / QA Agent
tasks.goal: Use TDD to keep runtime, authority, MVP bundle, LPS proof, proof-ledger, and task-status projections mutually consistent when an upstream verification gate fails, including null-safe identity handling and summaries that never claim pass while the structured result is fail or blocked.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests reproduce the strict-link failure cascade; every generated report remains schema-valid; absent identities are represented without fabricated None values; LPS-007 and LPS-008 summaries match blocked structured results; the task-status projection exposes proof invalidation; after ARCHBP-033 passes, rerun restores pass only from new evidence while ledger history remains append-only.
tasks.rollback_plan: Preserve all failure records, restore the prior generators, append an invalidation or recovery event rather than rewriting ledger history, and keep affected tasks blocked until every projection agrees.
tasks.next_action: Write failing cascade, contradictory-summary, null-identity, ledger-invalidation, and recovery tests before changing projection code.

tasks.title: Implement byte-complete host ALL-data import and reconstruction
tasks.owner_agent: PostgreSQL Agent / CodeDB Agent / Recovery Agent
tasks.goal: Use TDD to import every declared host data class as original bytes plus typed records into PostgreSQL/RuVector, preserve complete provenance and transformations, and prove zero-undeclared-loss reconstruction without promoting hashes or derived indexes above source bytes.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests cover zero-length, binary, invalid UTF-8, sparse, symlink, xattr, model, repository, log, cache, and protected-encrypted-secret fixtures; every original byte and transformation is queryable; export and reconstruction prove explicit byte, structure, metadata, semantic, and provenance equality with zero unclassified loss.
tasks.rollback_plan: Drop only task-created disposable schemas and fixtures by receipt, retain original inputs and failure evidence, and leave canonical cutover disabled.
tasks.next_action: Begin after ARCHBP-001 proves exact ingestion; enumerate the complete data-class corpus and write failing byte/metadata reconstruction tests.

tasks.title: Build the single-owner redb service and atomic mmap live projection
tasks.goal: Use TDD to give one supervised service the only writable redb handle, expose versioned authenticated Unix-domain mutation/query commands, and atomically publish checksummed read-only mmap generations plus ordered commit notifications for LifeOS without a second database opener, HTTP server, or PostgreSQL polling.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests prove single-opener denial, authenticated/versioned UDS requests, monotonic local_seq, inactive-slot write/fsync/checksum/atomic flip, matching ordered events, reconnect gaps, crash replay, spool preservation, corruption fallback, and benchmarked commit-to-render latency without data loss.
tasks.rollback_plan: Stop the task-created owner, remove only disposable test files and mmap regions by receipt, replay the preserved outbox, and keep live projection disabled.
tasks.next_action: After ARCHBP-002 defines the outbox seam, write failing single-opener, atomic-publication, replay, and stale-reader tests.

tasks.title: Implement and pin the byte-first rtk_nu adapter
tasks.goal: Use TDD to build the missing FlexNetOS rtk_nu adapter that tees raw stdout, stderr, and binary frames before transformation, links rtk compact views, and emits versioned JSONL, JSON, or Nuon envelopes without pretending to emit native Nu values or invoke the plugin itself.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests prove ordered raw teeing for stdout/stderr/binary, invalid UTF-8, partial lines, signals, nonzero exits, parser failure, large-stream chunking, digest and offset integrity, compact-view linkage, and exact profile-owned package resolution.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove the task-created adapter package and profile input, restore the prior RTK closure, retain raw fixtures and receipts, and keep structured legacy ingestion blocked.
tasks.next_action: Write failing envelope-schema, raw-order, binary, invalid-UTF-8, signal, parser-failure, and profile-resolution tests.

tasks.title: Enforce envctl-only durable commit and bidirectional reconciliation
tasks.owner_agent: envctl Agent / PostgreSQL Agent / Security Agent
tasks.goal: Use TDD to make envctl the exclusive production PostgreSQL/RuVector committer, drain ordered redb records with stable idempotency keys, orchestrate approved embeddings, attach transaction/LSN/generation/witness receipts, and project database-controlled state back through CodeDB and the redb owner.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests deny direct plugin/agent writes, prove envctl-only grants, ordered and idempotent drain/embed/commit/restart, exact commit identity and witness attachment, no acknowledgement before commit, and deterministic database-to-redb return projection.
tasks.rollback_plan: Revoke task-created roles and grants, stop the worker, replay from the last acknowledged cursor, remove only disposable namespaces, and retain complete attempt/error evidence.
tasks.next_action: After the owner and envelope seams pass, write failing privilege-bypass, crash-boundary, idempotency, acknowledgement-order, and return-projection tests.

tasks.title: Cut over the PostgreSQL RuVector operational runtime and retire transitional authorities
tasks.owner_agent: Governance Board / Runtime Kernel / Release Agent
tasks.goal: Perform the proof-gated final cutover only after the complete anchor conformance suite passes: PostgreSQL/RuVector becomes the Swarm Primary Runtime and host ALL-data owner, external surfaces remain controlled executors, transitional durable redb classes migrate by verified parity, and no parallel planning, storage, projection, or release authority survives.
tasks.verification_gate: Owner approval names the exact generation; every anchor gate passes; all durable classes and protected encrypted bytes are present and reconstructable; redb is subordinate and parity-migrated; external executors return complete evidence; old authorities are recoverably retired; activation, restart, rollback, PITR, and full reconstruction drills pass.
tasks.rollback_plan: Reselect the exact prior activation, restore archived authority inputs by manifest, replay transitional buffers, and retain both cutover and rollback evidence; never delete source or rollback bytes.
tasks.next_action: Remain blocked until ARCHBP-047 proves the complete topology; then prepare the exact owner-approved cutover and rollback runbook.
